chapter_title.en: Chapter 90 – The shade for the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/304)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/304)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ عَلِيَّ بْنَ شِهَابٍ يَشْكُو رَأْسَهُ وَ الْبَرْدُ شَدِيدٌ وَ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ كَمَا زَعَمَ فَلْيُظَلِّلْ وَ أَمَّا أَنْتَ فَاضْحَ لِمَنْ أَحْرَمْتَ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad, from Usman Bin Isa Al Kilaby who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassanasws the 1st that Ali Bin Shihaab complained about his headache and the intense cold, and he intends to wear Ihraam. So heasws said: ‘If it was as you are claiming, so let him shade (himself). And as for you, so brightness is for the Oneazwj Whom you are wearing Ihraam for’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ahmad has narrated from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa al-Kilabiy who has said the following: “I once said to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that Ali ibn Shihab has a headache, and cold is severe and he wants to assume the state of Ihram. He (the Imam) said, ‘If that is the case he can use shadows, but you must stay out of the shadow for the sake of the One for whom you assume the state of Ihram.’”
